# FEAT-3: Surface Sitecap Capture Duration in Generated Runbook Header

## Context

`discover()` already writes a header comment on generated runbooks:
```
# Generated by sitetest.discover() — 11 pages in 0.4s (capture time not included). Do not edit by hand.
```

Sitecap now returns `duration_ms` in its `meta.json` per page (merged via sitecap PR #31). FEAT-3 wires that through so the header includes capture time:
```
# Generated by sitetest.discover() — 11 pages, capture: 47.2s, discovery: 0.4s. Do not edit by hand.
```

## Design Decisions

### Sum per-page durations for total capture time

Each page's `meta.json` has `duration_ms`. Sum all of them to get total capture time. This is wall-clock time per page, not total wall-clock (sitecap may run pages concurrently), but it's the best signal available without sitecap exposing session-level timing.

### Graceful fallback when duration_ms is missing

Old sitecap captures won't have `duration_ms`. When missing, omit capture time from the header entirely — fall back to current format with `(capture time not included)`.

| pages with duration_ms | header format |
|---|---|
| All pages have it | `capture: 47.2s, discovery: 0.4s` |
| Some pages have it | `capture: ~32.1s, discovery: 0.4s` (tilde indicates partial) |
| No pages have it | `discovery: 0.4s (capture time not included)` |

### Accumulate duration alongside existing meta.json read

`discover()` already reads `meta.json` at line 244 for the URL. Add `duration_ms` extraction in the same `try` block — no second file read needed.

## Files to Modify

### `lib/discover.js`

**In the per-page loop** (line 244 `try` block): extract `meta.duration_ms` if present, accumulate into a `totalCaptureDuration` counter and a `pagesWithDuration` counter.

**After the loop** (line 272-274): build the header string based on the decision table above. Replace the current hardcoded `(capture time not included)` suffix.

### `test/discover.test.js`

**Update existing test fixture** (line 153): add `duration_ms: 1234` to the `meta.json` written in `beforeEach`.

**Update "discovers elements" test**: assert the header comment includes `capture:` and `discovery:` timing.

**New test: "header omits capture time when meta.json lacks duration_ms"**: write `meta.json` without `duration_ms`, assert header contains `(capture time not included)`.

**Test assertions:**
- `assert.ok(yamlContent.startsWith("# Generated by sitetest.discover()"))` (existing, implicit)
- `assert.match(yamlContent, /capture: \d+\.\d+s/)` — when duration_ms present
- `assert.match(yamlContent, /discovery: \d+\.\d+s/)` — always
- `assert.match(yamlContent, /capture time not included/)` — when duration_ms absent

## Dependency Direction

```
discover.js reads meta.json (written by sitecap) — no import change
```

No new modules, no new imports. Pure data extraction from an existing file read.

## Validation

### Automated (local + CI)

- `make check` — lint + all tests
- Existing discover tests updated to verify header format
- New test for fallback when `duration_ms` missing
- No browser tests needed — discover operates on sitecap output directories, not live pages

### Manual

- None required.

## Agent Team

Recommended: No — single file change + test updates, no parallelism opportunity.

## Before Closing

- [ ] Run `make check` (lint + tests pass)
- [ ] Verify header format matches spec for all three cases (all/some/no duration_ms)
- [ ] Verify existing discover tests still pass with updated meta.json fixture
- [ ] Verify `yaml.load()` on generated file still works (header is a YAML comment, not parsed)
